1	# Claude Code Rules for Flux
2	
3	## Git & GitHub
4	
5	- **Never reference Claude in commits or PRs.** Do not include "Co-Authored-By: Claude" or any mention of Claude/AI in commit messages, PR titles, or PR descriptions.
6	- **All changes must be submitted as pull requests.** Never push directly to the main branch. Always create a feature branch and open a PR for review.
7	- **Never merge PRs.** Only the user merges pull requests. Do not use `gh pr merge` or any merge commands.
8	
9	## Environment Management
10	
11	- **Always use nix for environment management.** This project uses nix flakes. Do not use `npm install` directly.
12	- **Default dev shell:** `nix develop` - for general development with Node.js and serve
13	- **Test shell:** `nix develop .#test` - includes Playwright browsers for e2e tests
14	- **Run tests:** `nix develop .#test --command npm test`
15

file.content:
# Claude Code Rules for Flux

## Git & GitHub

- **Never reference Claude in commits or PRs.** Do not include "Co-Authored-By: Claude" or any mention of Claude/AI in commit messages, PR titles, or PR descriptions.
- **All changes must be submitted as pull requests.** Never push directly to the main branch. Always create a feature branch and open a PR for review.
- **Never merge PRs.** Only the user merges pull requests. Do not use `gh pr merge` or any merge commands.

## Environment Management

- **Always use nix for environment management.** This project uses nix flakes. Do not use `npm install` directly.
- **Default dev shell:** `nix develop` - for general development with Node.js and serve
- **Test shell:** `nix develop .#test` - includes Playwright browsers for e2e tests
- **Run tests:** `nix develop .#test --command npm test`

input.old_string:
# Claude Code Rules for Flux

## Git & GitHub
input.new_string:
# Claude Code Rules for Flux

## Product context

Flux is the **workouts** surface of a three-app wellness toolset driven by an AI coach. The siblings are **vibe** (meditation, native Android, separate repo) and **balance** (check-ins / tracking, `gunk-dev/balance`, Capacitor + React, syncs via Google Sheets).

The coach generates programs, reviews progress, adapts to mood / sickness / stress signals from check-ins, and schedules reminders. Flux is one of its expressions — design and architecture decisions should be evaluated against the coaching relationship, not against flux as a standalone app.

## Git & GitHub

tool_use_result.originalFile:
# Claude Code Rules for Flux

## Git & GitHub

- **Never reference Claude in commits or PRs.** Do not include "Co-Authored-By: Claude" or any mention of Claude/AI in commit messages, PR titles, or PR descriptions.
- **All changes must be submitted as pull requests.** Never push directly to the main branch. Always create a feature branch and open a PR for review.
- **Never merge PRs.** Only the user merges pull requests. Do not use `gh pr merge` or any merge commands.

## Environment Management

- **Always use nix for environment management.** This project uses nix flakes. Do not use `npm install` directly.
- **Default dev shell:** `nix develop` - for general development with Node.js and serve
- **Test shell:** `nix develop .#test` - includes Playwright browsers for e2e tests
- **Run tests:** `nix develop .#test --command npm test`

lines: +Flux is the **workouts** surface of a three-app wellness toolset driven by an AI coach. The siblings are **vibe** (meditation, native Android, separate repo) and **balance** (check-ins / tracking, `gunk-dev/balance`, Capacitor + React, syncs via Google Sheets).

+The coach generates programs, reviews progress, adapts to mood / sickness / stress signals from check-ins, and schedules reminders. Flux is one of its expressions — design and architecture decisions should be evaluated against the coaching relationship, not against flux as a standalone app.
- **Never reference Claude in commits or PRs.** Do not include "Co-Authored-By: Claude" or any mention of Claude/AI in commit messages, PR titles, or PR descriptions.

+
+The coach generates programs, reviews progress, adapts to mood / sickness / stress signals from check-ins, and schedules reminders. Flux is one of its expressions — design and architecture decisions should be evaluated against the coaching relationship, not against flux as a standalone app.
+
 ## Git & GitHub
 
 - **Never reference Claude in commits or PRs.** Do not include "Co-Authored-By: Claude" or any mention of Claude/AI in commit messages, PR titles, or PR descriptions.

content.text:
PR opened: https://github.com/gunk-dev/flux/pull/79

Added a 5-line **Product context** section right after the title, framing flux as the workouts surface of the wellness toolset (alongside vibe + balance) driven by an AI coach. No other sections changed.
